citation: FinanceRateCalc Research. (2026). FRC Signals. financeratecalc.com/signals.json. CC BY 4.0.

active_signals.name: Conservative DTI Compression
active_signals.description: Large national banks reducing DTI caps from 50% toward 43-47%. Borrowers in 45-50% DTI range face elevated overlay denial risk at conservative lenders.
active_signals.recommendation: Route 45-50% DTI borrowers to FHA-focused or portfolio lenders.

active_signals.name: Non-QM Loosening
active_signals.description: Portfolio and Non-QM lenders reducing bank statement history requirement from 24 months toward 12 months. Rate premium vs agency products narrowing.
active_signals.recommendation: 12-month 1099 history now sufficient at many Non-QM programs.

active_signals.name: SSDI Acceptance Recovery
active_signals.description: Fair lending scrutiny driving SSDI gross-up acceptance from 52% (Q4 2024) to 71% (Q2 2026). Sustained multi-quarter improvement.
active_signals.recommendation: SSDI borrowers have more lender options than 18 months ago. Still verify gross-up application before applying.

active_signals.name: VA Residual Income Strength
active_signals.description: VA specialist lenders holding near agency minimums. Residual income methodology providing effective override for high-DTI veterans.
active_signals.recommendation: VA-eligible borrowers with high DTI should seek VA specialist lenders using residual income methodology, not simple DTI cap.

archived_signals.name: 2025 Peak Tightening
archived_signals.resolution: Rate expectation shift Q3 2025 drove overlay loosening.
